name: Interpretation Forecast review for Resolution Pathway And Tissue Repair bundle
summary: The staged outlook is calibrated for publication review: low-confidence human_biomarker_signal is supported by human lipid-mediator, leukocyte, cytokine, and disease-adjacent wound-biomarker records, while direct RvD1/MaR1 repair evidence remains animal-stage support. The review leaves a non-blocking human-judgment verdict because final public interpretation depends on curator weighting of precursor fish-oil/SPM products, direct SPM animal repair studies, and a completed older-adult venous-ulcer trial without posted results.

findings.claim_or_issue: The stage assignment uses human target-engagement and biomarker evidence plus a chronic venous leg ulcer repair-adjacent pilot, but deliberately stops at human_biomarker_signal.
findings.why_it_matters: A higher stage would over-read biomarker movement and disease-adjacent wound trends as general human tissue-repair or healthy-aging functional benefit.
findings.recommended_action: No revision required. Keep the current low-confidence human_biomarker_signal stage unless later surveillance finds controlled human repair or functional endpoints.

findings.claim_or_issue: RvD1 and MaR1 provide direct aged-tissue repair support in mouse muscle and bone models.
findings.why_it_matters: These studies strengthen biological plausibility for the track, but they do not justify a human functional-benefit stage without direct human intervention outcomes.
findings.recommended_action: No revision required. Keep animal repair records as mechanistic support beneath the human biomarker baseline.

findings.claim_or_issue: Human SPM Active and enriched marine-oil records are product/formulation-specific target-engagement evidence.
findings.why_it_matters: Conflating those products with pure direct SPM pharmacology would inflate the intervention class and obscure fish-oil precursor mechanisms.
findings.recommended_action: No revision required. Preserve the staged product and precursor boundaries during editorial approval.

findings.claim_or_issue: The staged outlook assigns steady momentum while carrying completed registry context for NCT03576989 without posted results.
findings.why_it_matters: This is conservative: completed trials without posted results should remain surveillance signals, not evidence of accelerating translational progress.
findings.recommended_action: No revision required. Keep steady momentum until posted results or new active trials add direct human repair endpoints.
